---
title: "nitrix.signal.tsconv"
generated: true
signature-hash: 8a7e416600885156
description: "1-D time-series convolution along the trailing axis."
listing:
  id: nitrix-signal-tsconv-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-signal-tsconv/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.signal.tsconv`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
1-D time-series convolution along the trailing axis.

A thin batched wrapper around `lax.conv_general_dilated` for
1-D temporal convolution with the typical fMRI / time-series
layout `(..., channels, observations)`.

What the legacy `hypercoil.functional.tsconv` had that we drop:

- `tsconv2d` -- "2D conv where one axis is time" via separate
  `lax.conv_general_dilated` calls.  Users wanting that should
  call `lax.conv_general_dilated` directly with the explicit
  dimension-numbers spec; wrapping it doesn't save code.
- `basisconv2d`, `polyconv2d`, `basischan`, `polychan` --
  scientific-question-specific (basis-function / polynomial-channel
  construction for HRF modelling).  Live elsewhere when they live
  again (probably `hypercoil`-side or a downstream consumer's
  utility module).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `C` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `obs` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `C_out` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `K` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `obs_out` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-signal-tsconv-symbols}
:::
